mission.title: emissary-ingress: Allow ambassador Helm chart to use CRDS when namespaced
mission.description: Allow ambassador Helm chart to use CRDS when namespaced . Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current emissary-ingress deployment
steps.description:
Verify your emissary-ingress version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n emissary-ingress -l app.kubernetes.io/name=emissary-ingress
helm list -n emissary-ingress 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working emissary-ingress installation.

steps.title: Review emissary-ingress configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant emissary-ingress configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n emissary-ingress -l app.kubernetes.io/name=emissary-ingress
kubectl get configmap -n emissary-ingress -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=emissary-ingress
```
I have a ambassador instance deployed as a namespaced resource and get the following 

```
2019-05-29 08:34:51 kubewatch [32 TMainThread] 0.70.1 DEBUG: looking up ID for namespace god-default
2019-05-29 08:34:51 kubewatch [32 TMainThread] 0.70.1

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow ambassador Helm chart to use CRDS when namespaced
steps.description:
So here's the issue: suppose that you have a new Ambassador user who sets up a bunch of stuff with CRDs but doesn't quite get it right. If they forget to create the CRD types at all, that's not so bad: Kubernetes won't let them `kubectl apply` the CRDs and they'll see that something is wrong.

If
```yaml
2019-05-29 08:34:51 kubewatch [32 TMainThread] 0.70.1 DEBUG: looking up ID for namespace god-default
2019-05-29 08:34:51 kubewatch [32 TMainThread] 0.70.1 DEBUG: CRD type definition unreadable for authservices.getambassador.io: Forbidden
2019-05-29 08:34:51 kubewatch [32 TMainThread] 0.70.1 DEBUG: CRD type definition unreadable for mappings.getambassador.io: Forbidden
2019-05-29 08:34:51 kubewatch [32 TMainThread] 0.70.1 DEBUG: CRD type definition unreadable for modules.getambassador.io: Forbidden
2019-05-29 08:34:51 kubewatch [32 TMainThread] 0.70.1 DEBUG: CRD type definition unreadable for r
```

steps.title: Upgrade emissary-ingress to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade emissary-ingress:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade emissary-ingress emissary-ingress/emissary-ingress --namespace emissary-ingress
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n emissary-ingress
helm list -n emissary-ingress
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n emissary-ingress -l app.kubernetes.io/name=emissary-ingress
kubectl get events -n emissary-ingress --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow ambassador Helm chart to use CRDS when namespaced " is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
So here's the issue: suppose that you have a new Ambassador user who sets up a bunch of stuff with CRDs but doesn't quite get it right. If they forget to create the CRD types at all, that's not so bad: Kubernetes won't let them `kubectl apply` the CRDs and they'll see that something is wrong.

If they get RBAC wrong, though, that's much more annoying.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with emissary-ingress installed or the issue environment reproducible.
